name: EDRaser
summary:
EDRaser is a powerful tool for remotely deleting access logs, Windows event logs, databases, and other files on remote machines.
It offers two modes of operation: automated and manual.

documentation.readme: EDRaser is a powerful tool for remotely deleting access logs, Windows event logs, databases, and other files on remote machines. It offers two modes of operation: automated and manual.
usage_sections: Remote deletion of web server logs.
Local deletion of Windows Application event logs.
Remote deletion of Windows event logs.
ip: scan IP addresses in the specified range and attack vulnerable systems (default: localhost).

arguments.name: -auto
arguments.description: Automatically scan the curernt C LAN network and perform every possible attack

arguments.name: -db_password
arguments.description: A password for remote connection to database (used in remote_db_attack)

arguments.name: -db_username
arguments.description: A username for remote connection to database (used in remote_db_attack)

arguments.name: -ip
arguments.description: A IP address for remote attack (default: localhost)

arguments.name: -port
arguments.description: Port number for remote connection to DB / WebServer / EventLog

arguments.name: -sigfile
arguments.description: A Path to the encrypted signatures file

function_signatures.name: run_access_logs_attack
function_signatures.signature: def run_access_logs_attack(ip: str, port: int = 80, log_insertion: int = 10)

function_signatures.name: run_local_database_attack
function_signatures.signature: def run_local_database_attack(db_name: str, table_name: str , signature_DB: list)

function_signatures.name: run_remote_database_attack
function_signatures.signature: def run_remote_database_attack(signature_DB: list ,  DB_type: str , username: str , password: str, host: str, port: int, DB_name: str = None, table_name: str = None)

function_signatures.name: report_event_to_event_log
function_signatures.signature:
def report_event_to_event_log(log_name: str,
                              event_type: int,
                              event_category: int,
                              event_id: int,
                              sid: pywintypes.SIDType = None,
                              event_description: List[str] = None,
                              data: str = None)

function_signatures.name: run_remote_security_event_log_deletion
function_signatures.signature: def run_remote_security_event_log_deletion(signatureDB: List[Signature], server_ip: str, port: int = 445, log_insertions: int = 20000)

function_signatures.name: run_web_server
function_signatures.signature: def run_web_server(DB_server_ip: str, db_type: str, port: int, db_username: str, db_password: str, table_name: str)

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `python edraser.py --auto`. Further invocations from upstream: `python edraser.py --ip <ip_addr> --attack <attack_name> [--sigfile <signature file>]`; `python edraser.py --attack windows_event_log --ip $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. EDRaser is a powerful tool for remotely deleting access logs, Windows event logs, databases, and other files on remote machines. It offers two modes of operation: automated and manual.
documentation.when_to_use:
Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: EDRaser is a powerful tool for remotely deleting access logs, Windows event logs, databases, and other files on remote machines.
It offers two modes of operation: automated and manual.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: EDRaser is a powerful tool for remotely deleting access logs, Windows event logs, databases, and other files on remote machines. It offers two modes of operation: automated and manual.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases:
When you need Penetration Testing, use EDRaser because EDRaser is a powerful tool for remotely deleting access logs, Windows event logs, databases, and other files on remote machines.
It offers two modes of operatio.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python edraser.py --auto` as documented upstream for EDRaser.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python edraser.py --ip <ip_addr> --attack <attack_name> [--sigfile <signature file>]` as documented upstream for EDRaser.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python edraser.py --attack windows_event_log --ip $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST` as documented upstream for EDRaser.

When you need a concrete invocation, run `python EDRaser.py -attack remote_db -db_type mysql -db_username test_user -db_password=$KFIOSA_SECRET -ip $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST` as documented upstream for EDRaser.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair EDRaser with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `EDRaser --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
